data.text: I’m reading the reachability evidence and the referenced GraphQL files first, then I’ll decide symbol-by-symbol whether each one is wired into production or should be deleted. If the scope stays within the current package set, I’ll make the smallest code change, write the decisions log, run the required checks, and commit once.

data.text: The execution worktree is not at the Go module root, so I’m locating the actual repository layout and the GraphQL package before I touch anything. After that I’ll read the files that define the five symbols and the deadcode evidence around them.

input.command: /bin/zsh -lc "find . -maxdepth 3 -type d | sed -n '1,120p' && printf '\\n---\\n' && rg -n \"module |package graphql|func NewResolver|func personaConnectionFrom|func resetProviderModelsCacheForTest|func RecordHarnessRateLimit|func resetHarnessRateLimitCache\" -g'*.go' ."

data.text: I have the resolver signatures now. Next I’m tracing each symbol from the package outward to see whether it has a production entrypoint, a test-only helper role, or is truly dead.

data.output:
1	package graphql
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"sync"
     6		"time"
     7	
     8		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
     9	)
    10	
    11	// workingDirKey is the context key used to thread a per-request WorkingDir
    12	// through GraphQL resolvers. LAYER 2 of the GraphQL multi-project fix
    13	// (ddx-055e8d32) lets the scoped /api/projects/{project}/graphql route inject
    14	// the resolved project's WorkingDir into the request context so the
    15	// singleton resolver constructed at server start can serve any project
    16	// without per-request reconstruction.
    17	type workingDirKey struct{}
    18	
    19	// WithWorkingDir returns ctx with workingDir attached for downstream resolver
    20	// access. The server's GraphQL HTTP handler MUST call this before delegating
    21	// to the gqlgen handler so resolvers read the request-scoped project root
    22	// rather than the resolver struct's fallback default.
    23	func WithWorkingDir(ctx context.Context, workingDir string) context.Context {
    24		if workingDir == "" {
    25			return ctx
    26		}
    27		return context.WithValue(ctx, workingDirKey{}, workingDir)
    28	}
    29	
    30	// WorkingDirFromContext returns the WorkingDir previously attached via
    31	// WithWorkingDir, or the empty string if none was set. Callers should prefer
    32	// (*Resolver).workingDir(ctx) which falls back to the resolver's default.
    33	func WorkingDirFromContext(ctx context.Context) string {
    34		if ctx == nil {
    35			return ""
    36		}
    37		if v, ok := ctx.Value(workingDirKey{}).(string); ok {
    38			return v
    39		}
    40		return ""
    41	}
    42	
    43	// workingDir returns the request-scoped WorkingDir (via WithWorkingDir) when
    44	// present, falling back to r.WorkingDir. All resolver methods with access to
    45	// ctx should call this rather than reading r.WorkingDir directly so the same
    46	// resolver instance can serve multiple projects safely.
    47	func (r *Resolver) workingDir(ctx context.Context) string {
    48		if dir := WorkingDirFromContext(ctx); dir != "" {
    49			return dir
    50		}
    51		return r.WorkingDir
    52	}
    53	
    54	// BeadLifecycleSubscriber can subscribe to live lifecycle events from a bead store.
    55	// bead.WatcherHub satisfies this interface.
    56	type BeadLifecycleSubscriber interface {
    57		SubscribeLifecycle(projectID string) (<-chan bead.LifecycleEvent, func())
    58	}
    59	
    60	// ExecuteLoopWaker signals running execute-loop workers bound to a project
    61	// to skip their idle-poll sleep and re-scan the ready queue. The
    62	// operatorPromptApprove and operatorPromptSubmit (auto-approve) resolvers
    63	// call WakeProject after a successful proposed → open transition so the
    64	// bead is claimed without waiting for the next poll tick. The server's
    65	// WorkerManager satisfies this interface; tests inject a stub to assert
    66	// the wake call without spinning a real worker.
    67	type ExecuteLoopWaker interface {
    68		WakeProject(projectRoot string) int
    69	}
    70	
    71	// ActionDispatcher starts backend workers for GraphQL action mutations.
    72	// The server package supplies the production implementation so this package
    73	// does not import the outer server package.
    74	type ActionDispatcher interface {
    75		DispatchWorker(ctx context.Context, kind string, projectRoot string, args *string) (*WorkerDispatchResult, error)
    76		DispatchPlugin(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, name string, action string, scope string) (*PluginDispatchResult, error)
    77		StopWorker(ctx context.Context, id string) (*WorkerLifecycleResult, error)
    78	}
    79	
    80	type Resolver struct {
    81		State      StateProvider
    82		WorkingDir string
    83		Workers    ProgressSubscriber
    84		BeadBus    BeadLifecycleSubscriber
    85		Actions    ActionDispatcher
    86		// ExecLogs provides execution run log retrieval for the executionEvidence
    87		// subscription. If nil, that subscription returns an error.
    88		ExecLogs ExecLogProvider
    89		// CoordMetrics provides coordinator metrics snapshots for the
    90		// coordinatorMetrics subscription. If nil, that subscription returns an error.
    91		CoordMetrics CoordinatorMetricsProvider
    92		// MetricsPollInterval controls how often CoordinatorMetrics polls for
    93		// changes. Defaults to 1 second when zero.
    94		MetricsPollInterval time.Duration
    95		// CSRFTokens validates the X-CSRF-Token header on operatorPromptSubmit
    96		// (and other future write mutations that require CSRF protection).
    97		// Nil here causes operatorPromptSubmit to reject every call with the
    98		// CSRF error rather than failing open.
    99		CSRFTokens CSRFTokenStore
   100		// OperatorPromptIdempotency deduplicates operatorPromptSubmit calls by
   101		// idempotency key within a 24-hour window. Server callers must wire a
   102		// process-wide instance (NewMemoryIdempotencyCache).
   103		OperatorPromptIdempotency IdempotencyCache
   104		// OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist is the per-project list of localhost
   105		// identity actors that may auto-approve their own operator-prompt
   106		// submissions (operatorPromptSubmit input.autoApprove=true) and that may
   107		// invoke operatorPromptApprove. The locked Story 15 decision restricts
   108		// approval to configured-localhost identities — ts-net identities are
   109		// NEVER eligible regardless of the allowlist contents. The literal
   110		// sentinel "localhost" in the list matches any localhost actor; otherwise
   111		// the entry must equal the actor string produced by operatorPromptIdentity
   112		// (e.g. "localhost:127.0.0.1:55812"). An empty allowlist disables both
   113		// auto-approve and the manual approve mutation for this project.
   114		OperatorPromptAutoApproveAllowlist []string
   115		// PromptCapBytes caps the prompt body size accepted by
   116		// operatorPromptSubmit. When zero the resolver falls back to
   117		// DefaultOperatorPromptCapBytes (= evidence.DefaultMaxPromptBytes), which
   118		// matches the inline-prompt cap on /api/agent/run.
   119		PromptCapBytes int
   120		// BuildSHA is the server build commit recorded on the operator-prompt
   121		// audit event so the immutable first event captures the binary version
   122		// that accepted the submission. Empty -> "unknown".
   123		BuildSHA string
   124		// NodeID is the receiving server's stable node ID, captured on the
   125		// operator-prompt audit event. Used together with the originating
   126		// X-Tailscale-Node header (which identifies the *peer*'s node) so the
   127		// audit trail records both ends of the trust attestation.
   128		NodeID string
   129		// ExecuteLoopWaker, when non-nil, is signalled by the operator-prompt
   130		// approve / auto-approve resolvers immediately after a successful
   131		// proposed → open transition so a running execute-loop worker bound to
   132		// the project drops its idle-poll sleep and claims the bead in the
   133		// current tick. Nil → resolver simply skips the wake (the next tick
   134		// will pick the bead up after PollInterval).
   135		ExecuteLoopWaker ExecuteLoopWaker
   136		// Federation, when non-nil, supplies the spoke registry and fan-out
   137		// client used by the federationNodes / federated{Beads,Runs,Projects}
   138		// query resolvers. Nil → those queries return empty lists (the default
   139		// for non-hub servers).
   140		Federation FederationProvider
   141		// RunRequeueIdempotency deduplicates runRequeue mutation calls by
   142		// idempotency key. Concurrent and repeat calls with the same key return
   143		// the same originating bead and never enqueue a second time. Server
   144		// callers wire a process-wide instance (NewMemoryIdempotencyCache); when
   145		// nil, the resolver returns an error rather than silently failing open.
   146		RunRequeueIdempotency IdempotencyCache
   147		// runRequeueMu serializes the lookup+reserve+store steps of the
   148		// runRequeue mutation so that concurrent calls with the same
   149		// idempotencyKey collapse to a single re-queue (atomic claims).
   150		runRequeueMu sync.Mutex
   151		// ReportedWorkers, when non-nil, supplies the worker_ingest derived view
   152		// for the reportedWorkers query. Nil → resolver returns an empty list.
   153		ReportedWorkers ReportedWorkersProvider
   154	}
   155	
   156	// ReportedWorkersProvider returns a snapshot of the worker_ingest derived
   157	// view (ADR-022 rev 5 §Worker-server interface), already classified by
   158	// freshness state. The server package supplies the production implementation.
   159	type ReportedWorkersProvider interface {
   160		GetReportedWorkers() []*ReportedWorker
   161	}
   162	
   163	// Mutation returns MutationResolver implementation.
   164	func (r *Resolver) Mutation() MutationResolver { return &mutationResolver{r} }
   165	
   166	// Query returns QueryResolver implementation.
   167	func (r *Resolver) Query() QueryResolver { return &queryResolver{r} }
   168	
   169	// Subscription returns SubscriptionResolver implementation.
   170	func (r *Resolver) Subscription() SubscriptionResolver { return &subscriptionResolver{r} }
   171	
   172	type mutationResolver struct{ *Resolver }
   173	type queryResolver struct{ *Resolver }
   174	type artifactResolver struct{ *Resolver }
   175	type subscriptionResolver struct{ *Resolver }

data.text: I’ve confirmed the provider-cache helpers and the rate-limit cache live in the same package. I’m checking whether any of these are only test scaffolding or whether production code is supposed to call them but currently doesn’t.

    18	}{

    20		inFlight: make(map[string]bool),
    21	}
    22	
    23	// harnessRateLimitCache holds the most recently observed rate-limit signal per
    24	// harness name. The server invocation path (or tests) populates it via
    25	// RecordHarnessRateLimit; quotaFromHarnessInfo reads it when the upstream
    26	// HarnessInfo.Quota does not carry token-level ceilings.
    27	var harnessRateLimitCache = struct {
    28		sync.RWMutex
    29		byName map[string]agent.RateLimitSignal
    30	}{byName: make(map[string]agent.RateLimitSignal)}
    31	
    32	// LookupHarnessRateLimit returns the last-observed signal for a harness, or
    33	// ok=false if none has been recorded.
    34	func LookupHarnessRateLimit(name string) (agent.RateLimitSignal, bool) {
    35		harnessRateLimitCache.RLock()
    36		defer harnessRateLimitCache.RUnlock()
    37		sig, ok := harnessRateLimitCache.byName[name]
    38		return sig, ok
    39	}
    40	
    41	// ProviderStatuses is the resolver for the providerStatuses field.
    42	// It mirrors the output of `ddx agent providers`, annotating each row with
    43	// kind=ENDPOINT, a lastCheckedAt timestamp, and rolling usage derived from
    44	// the sessions index. Quota is populated when the upstream ProviderInfo
    45	// exposes token-level quota headers; null otherwise (FEAT-014 no-fabrication).
    46	func (r *queryResolver) ProviderStatuses(ctx context.Context) ([]*ProviderStatus, error) {
    47		now := time.Now().UTC()
    48		entries := r.sessionIndexEntries(ctx)
    49	
    50		if rows := cachedProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx)); len(rows) > 0 {
    51			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    52			return providerRowsWithUsage(rows, entries, now), nil
    53		}
    54	
    55		if snapshots, ok, err := agent.ConfiguredProviderSnapshots(r.workingDir(ctx)); err == nil && ok {
    56			rows := providerStatusesFromInfos(snapshots, entries, now)
    57			storeProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx), rows)
    58			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    59			return rows, nil
    60		} else if err != nil {
    61			return nil, fmt.Errorf("loading provider snapshots: %w", err)
    62		}
    63	
    64		// Legacy/global provider config fallback. Bound the synchronous path so the
    65		// UI can still first-paint harness rows even if provider probing is slow.
    66		probeCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, 500*time.Millisecond)
    67		defer cancel()
    68		providers, err := liveProviderInfos(probeCtx, r.workingDir(ctx))
    69		if err != nil {
    70			refreshProviderStatuses(r.workingDir(ctx))
    71			return []*ProviderStatus{}, nil
    72		}
    73		rows := providerStatusesFromInfos(providers, entries, now)
    74		storeProviderRows(r.workingDir(ctx), rows)
    75		return rows, nil
    76	}
    77	
    78	// HarnessStatuses is the resolver for the harnessStatuses field.
    79	// It returns one row per subprocess harness (kind=HARNESS). Reachability is
    80	// taken from HarnessInfo.Available, rolling usage from the sessions index,

   490	// quotaFromProviderInfo derives a ProviderQuota from the upstream ProviderInfo.
   491	// Returns nil when no token-level ceiling is published.
   492	func quotaFromProviderInfo(p agentlib.ProviderInfo) *ProviderQuota {
   493		if p.Quota == nil {
   494			return nil
   495		}
   496		return quotaFromState(p.Quota)
   497	}
   498	
   499	// quotaFromHarnessInfo derives a ProviderQuota from the upstream HarnessInfo.
   500	// When a rate-limit signal has been captured for this harness (via
   501	// RecordHarnessRateLimit — typically on the server's harness-dispatch path
   502	// after parsing response headers with agent.ParseRateLimitHeaders), that
   503	// signal takes precedence because it carries absolute token ceilings. The
   504	// upstream HarnessInfo.Quota only exposes percent-used windows without an
   505	// absolute ceiling. Returns nil when nothing usable is published.
   506	func quotaFromHarnessInfo(info agentlib.HarnessInfo) *ProviderQuota {
   507		if sig, ok := LookupHarnessRateLimit(info.Name); ok {
   508			if q := QuotaFromRateLimitSignal(sig); q != nil {
   509				return q
   510			}
   511		}
   512		if info.Quota == nil {
   513			return nil
   514		}
   515		return quotaFromState(info.Quota)
   516	}
   517	
   518	// quotaFromState derives a ProviderQuota from an upstream QuotaState. The
   519	// upstream QuotaWindow doesn't expose absolute token ceilings (only percent
   520	// used), so we surface the window length and reset time only. Ceiling and
   521	// remaining stay unknown unless a harness-specific rate-limit-header path
   522	// populates them via QuotaFromRateLimitSignal.
   523	func quotaFromState(state *agentlib.QuotaState) *ProviderQuota {
   524		if state == nil || len(state.Windows) == 0 {

   526		}

   528		var resetAt string
   529		for _, w := range state.Windows {
